title: WSJ Investigation: ICE Agents Fired Into Civilian Vehicles 13 Times Since July 2025
excerpt: A Wall Street Journal investigation identified at least 13 incidents since July 2025 in which immigration agents fired at or into civilian vehicles, resulting in eight people shot and two deaths. Court records and attorneys indicated only one civilian was armed—with a concealed weapon never drawn—and at least five of those shot were U.S. citizens.
content:
Published in early January 2026, a Wall Street Journal investigation documented a striking pattern of ICE and CBP agents discharging weapons into civilian vehicles during the second Trump administration's immigration enforcement surge.

  • At least 13 incidents were identified from July 2025 through January 2026 in which immigration agents fired at or into civilian vehicles, resulting in 8 gunshot wounds and 2 deaths.
  • Court records and attorneys indicated only one of the civilians shot was armed—a concealed weapon that was never drawn.
  • At least five of those shot during this period were U.S. citizens.
  • Many U.S. police agencies, including DOJ-funded entities, train officers not to fire into moving vehicles or have banned the practice outside of rare circumstances such as a driver actively ramming a crowd.
  • Analysts noted an unusually large proportion of immigration agent shootings involving moving vehicles compared to other federal law enforcement.
The pattern raised questions about ICE use-of-force training, which does not explicitly prohibit firing at vehicle operators under all circumstances, unlike many major city police department policies.
